channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Artemis II arrives in lunar space ahead of its trip around the Moon - Engadget
item.description:
1. Artemis II arrives in lunar space ahead of its trip around the Moon  Engadget
  2. Artemis II Flight Day 6: Lunar Flyby Updates  nasa.gov
  3. Live updates: Artemis II astronauts break Apollo record on historic moon flyby  CNN
  4. Artemis II live updates: NASA's moon mission breaks Apollo record for farthest distance humans have traveled from Earth  Yahoo
  5. What time will Artemis II reach the moon? How to watch live  Cincinnati Enquirer

item.title: Toilet Troubles Haunting Artemis II Crew - The Daily Beast
item.description:
1. Toilet Troubles Haunting Artemis II Crew  The Daily Beast
  2. Artemis II Flight Update: Crew and Ground Teams Successfully Troubleshoot Orion’s Toilet  nasa.gov
  3. On NASA’s Moon Mission, the Biggest Headache Has Been the Toilet  Bloomberg.com
  4. Moon-bound Artemis II crew reflects on wonder of deep space experience: "You are special in all of this emptiness"  CBS News
  5. The breakout star of NASA's Artemis 2 moon mission isn't an astronaut — it's the space toilet  Space

item.title: Isaacman defends NASA budget proposal despite steep cuts - SpaceNews
item.description:
1. Isaacman defends NASA budget proposal despite steep cuts  SpaceNews
  2. On the brink of its greatest achievement in decades, NASA is facing science budget cuts  CNN
  3. White House seeks $5.6 billion cut to NASA budget in 2027  Reuters
  4. Deja vu: Trump proposes cutting NASA science funding by 47% again  Space
  5. NASA administrator defends Trump’s proposed budget cuts  The Hill

item.title: Scientists slam plan to control sunlight with mirrors in space - New York Post
item.description:
1. Scientists slam plan to control sunlight with mirrors in space  New York Post

  3. Are We About to Permanently Scar the Night Sky With One Million AI Satellite and 50,000 Space Mirrors?  Universe Today
  4. Circadian Rhythm Expert Talks Satellites with The Guardian  U of G News

item.title: Artemis 2 moon astronauts will try to recreate Apollo 8's historic 'Earthrise' photo during April 6 flyby - Space
item.description:
1. Artemis 2 moon astronauts will try to recreate Apollo 8's historic 'Earthrise' photo during April 6 flyby  Space
  2. 58 Years After ‘Earthrise,’ NASA’s New Moonshot May Rediscover Earth  The New York Times
  3. Something special is happening in space right now  Big Think
  4. Artemis II has 32 cameras, but recreating this iconic space photo still won’t be easy  Digital Camera World
  5. The Artemis II crew has a chance to recreate ‘Earthrise,’ one of the most famous photos of all time  National Geographic

item.title: China and Europe launch rare joint space mission - Financial Times
item.description:
1. China and Europe launch rare joint space mission  Financial Times
  2. How to follow the Smile launch live  European Space Agency
  3. New satellite to give global view on Earth’s ‘magnetic shield’, thanks to Leicester-built telescope  University of Leicester
  4. Mission “Smile”: Europe and China launch joint mission to study space weather  Table.Briefings
  5. China and Europe Launch SMILE — A Space Mission Aimed at Protecting the Global Economy From Solar Storms  europeanbusinessmagazine.com

item.title: NASA’s Moon ship and rocket seem to be working well, so what about the landers? - Ars Technica

item.title: The powerful new Rubin Observatory just found 11,000 new asteroids and measured 'tens of thousands more' - Space
item.description:
1. The powerful new Rubin Observatory just found 11,000 new asteroids and measured 'tens of thousands more'  Space
  2. 11,000 Asteroids Have Just Been Found — 33 Are Near-Earth Objects  Forbes
  3. The Rubin Observatory just turned the night sky into a live feed  Federal News Network
  4. The Vera C. Rubin Observatory Is About to Revolutionize Astronomy  ZME Science
  5. Rubin’s Eyes Wide Open  NOIRLab

item.title: Artemis II astronauts find hidden Easter eggs as they close in on the moon - Yahoo
item.description:
1. Artemis II astronauts find hidden Easter eggs as they close in on the moon  Yahoo
  2. NASA’s Artemis II mission nears its historic lunar flyby  scientificamerican.com

  4. Artemis II astronauts find hidden eggs on Easter Sunday as they close in on the moon  CBS News
  5. How Artemis 2’s Easter message echoes Apollo history  Astronomy Magazine

item.title: "Forbidden" Planet Too Big For Its Star Has An Atmosphere Like None We've Seen Before - IFLScience
item.description:
1. "Forbidden" Planet Too Big For Its Star Has An Atmosphere Like None We've Seen Before  IFLScience
  2. Cosmic Mysteries Swirl Around “Forbidden Planet”  Nautilus | Science

  4. Giant planet that shouldn’t exist is forcing astronomers to rethink planetary science  The Brighter Side of News

item.title: Newly discovered fossils give scientists first look at the evolution of early complex animals - PBS
item.description:
1. Newly discovered fossils give scientists first look at the evolution of early complex animals  PBS
  2. Your squishy, wormlike ancestors are at least 4 million years older than you thought  CNN
  3. Chinese fossils reveal a primordial burst of animal evolution  Reuters
  4. Fossils reveal many complex animals existed before the Cambrian explosion  Science News
  5. ‘Jaw-dropping’ fossils reset the clock on when complex animals evolved  scientificamerican.com

item.title: Bennu sample reveals how water flowed through the newly forming asteroid - Phys.org
item.description:
1. Bennu sample reveals how water flowed through the newly forming asteroid  Phys.org

  3. NASA’s asteroid Bennu sample reveals a hidden chemical patchwork  ScienceDaily
  4. Bennu Sample Reveals Three Distinct Organic–Mineral Domains Shaped by Ancient Water  Discover Magazine
  5. Bennu Sample Reveals Diverse Chemical Landscape Shaped by Ancient Water  National Today

item.title: Astronomers thought the early universe was full of hydrogen: Now they've found it - Phys.org
item.description:
1. Astronomers thought the early universe was full of hydrogen: Now they've found it  Phys.org

item.title: Exploding primordial black holes might have reshaped the early universe, and created all matter as we know it - Phys.org
item.description:
1. Exploding primordial black holes might have reshaped the early universe, and created all matter as we know it  Phys.org
  2. LIGO May Have Detected The First Primordial Black Hole, Scientists Say  ScienceAlert
  3. Rare Gravitational Wave Discovery May Hold the Key to First Evidence of Primordial Black Holes  The Daily Galaxy
  4. Did Scientists Just Detect an Exploding Black Hole?  physics.mit.edu
  5. Peculiar Gravitational Wave Collision Might Be First Evidence Of Primordial Black Holes  IFLScience
